message:
feat(types): extend CancelReason with attribution and cause chains

Implements asupersync-jl3i: Extend CancelReason with attribution.

Adds four new CancelKind variants:
- Deadline: Budget deadline exhaustion (§3.2.1)
- PollQuota: Poll quota exhaustion (§3.2.2)
- CostBudget: Cost budget exhaustion (§3.2.3)
- ResourceUnavailable: Resource unavailability

Extends CancelReason struct with full attribution:
- origin_region: RegionId that initiated the cancellation
- origin_task: Optional TaskId that initiated the cancellation
- timestamp: Time when cancellation was requested
- cause: Optional Box<CancelReason> for building cause chains

Adds helper methods for cause chain traversal:
- chain(): Iterator over the cause chain
- root_cause(): Returns the root cause at the end of the chain
- chain_depth(): Returns the depth of the cause chain
- any_cause_is(): Checks if any cause in the chain matches a kind
- caused_by(): Checks if a reason was caused by another
- is_kind(): Checks if reason's kind matches a given kind
- is_budget_exceeded(): True for Deadline/PollQuota/CostBudget
- is_time_exceeded(): True for Timeout/Deadline

Updates severity levels to accommodate new variants:
- 0: User
- 1: Timeout, Deadline
- 2: PollQuota, CostBudget
- 3: FailFast, RaceLost
- 4: ParentCancelled, ResourceUnavailable
- 5: Shutdown

Builder methods allow fluent construction:
- with_origin(), with_task(), with_message(), with_cause()
- with_timestamp(), with_region()

Backwards compatible: existing constructors like new(), timeout(),
shutdown() use testing defaults for attribution fields.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
